channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Two powerful earthquakes hit Venezuelan capital of Caracas
item.description: Powerful back to back earthquakes hit Venezuela's capital, Caracas. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with journalist Maria Graterol who is in Caracas.
item.content:encoded: Powerful back to back earthquakes hit Venezuela's capital, Caracas. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with journalist Maria Graterol who is in Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Trump cancels signing ceremony for bipartisan housing bill, again upending GOP agenda
item.description: Republicans hoped to celebrate passage of a bipartisan spending bill on Wednesday — until President Trump scuttled their plans. It was the latest in a series of rifts between Trump and his party.
item.content:encoded: Republicans hoped to celebrate passage of a bipartisan spending bill on Wednesday — until President Trump scuttled their plans. It was the latest in a series of rifts between Trump and his party.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: For vaccine information, some pediatricians are finding ways to sidestep Trump's CDC
item.description: The Trump administration's efforts to stop recommending some childhood vaccines has created a dilemma for pediatricians. Some are actively steering patients away from the CDC website.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration's efforts to stop recommending some childhood vaccines has created a dilemma for pediatricians. Some are actively steering patients away from the CDC website.
item.dc:creator: Karen Brown

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost a year after catastrophic Texas floods
item.description: Camp Mystic, the all-girls Christian Texas summer camp, where 28 people died during the catastrophic floods last year filed for bankruptcy reorganization on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Camp Mystic, the all-girls Christian Texas summer camp, where 28 people died during the catastrophic floods last year filed for bankruptcy reorganization on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: One year after Grand Canyon fire, an emotional re-opening
item.description: The biggest wildfire in America last year was at Grand Canyon National Park. The area that burned recently re-opened and recovery, of the land and emotions of people who loved it has begun.
item.content:encoded: The biggest wildfire in America last year was at Grand Canyon National Park. The area that burned recently re-opened and recovery, of the land and emotions of people who loved it has begun.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Skabelund

item.title: High gas prices hurt, even if you travel by rubber raft
item.description: River trips require car and truck shuttles to get floaters from the end back to where they started. High gas prices have some cancelling their plans.
item.content:encoded: River trips require car and truck shuttles to get floaters from the end back to where they started. High gas prices have some cancelling their plans.
item.dc:creator: Chris Clements

item.title: A volunteer otter watcher helped make a scientific discovery
item.description: A man who volunteered as an otter spotter made some scientific discoveries along the way.
item.content:encoded: A man who volunteered as an otter spotter made some scientific discoveries along the way.
item.dc:creator: Elena Neale-Sacks

item.title: Another top general expected to exit the Pentagon
item.description: The commander of U.S. Army forces in Europe and Africa is expected to announce his retirement. It's yet another abrupt move in the ongoing changes to senior Pentagon leadership under Pete Hegseth.
item.content:encoded: The commander of U.S. Army forces in Europe and Africa is expected to announce his retirement. It's yet another abrupt move in the ongoing changes to senior Pentagon leadership under Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Mayor Mamdani endorsed three House candidates. They all won
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Zohran Mamdani, the mayor of New York City, the day after all three Congressional candidates he endorsed won their Democratic primary elections.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Zohran Mamdani, the mayor of New York City, the day after all three Congressional candidates he endorsed won their Democratic primary elections.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Aslam

item.title: U.S. military works on building a better meal for the troops
item.description: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.content:encoded: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price
